content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
March 22, 1988 
Melvin G. Cooper, Executive Director 
Alabama state Ethics Commission 
817 South Court Street 
suite 2B Annex 
Montgomery, Alabama 36104 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-88-105 
Dear Mr. Cooper: 
You have asked whether, under our law, spouses of state 
legislators may be employed by special interest groups as paid 
lobbyists. 
Our law, the Political Reform Act (the "Act) 17, 
does not prohibit the spouse of a state legislator from being a 
paid lobbyist. 
However, the legislator would have certain 
financial disclosure and disqualification obligations under the 
Act's conflict-of-interest provisions (See Duffy Advice Letter, 
No. A-85-126, copy enclosed.) 
If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to 
contact me. 
sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
L

James J, Hicks, M.D. 
Cordell Wynn. Ph.D 
Sandra K. Martin, Ph.D, 
James H. Anderson. Esq. 
J. Ray Warren 
STATE OF ALABAMA 
STATE ETHICS COMMISSION 
817 SOUTH COURT STREET 
SUITE 2B ANNEX 
MONTGOMERY. ALABAMA 36104 
March 7, 1988 
Mr. John Keplinger, Director 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Mr. Keplinger: 
Once again, we need your help, and we need it fast. 
us quickly and answer this question: 
TELEPHONE (205) 261-2997 
Melvin G. Cooper 
Executive Director 
Howard E. McKenzie 
Assistant Director 
Write 
"Under your Ethics law, may spouses of State Legislators 
be employed by speci.al interests groups as paid lobbyists?" 
If you have Advisory Opinions or rulings relative to this 
question, we would greatly appreciate receiving copies of those 
documents. 
You have been mighty helpful to us in the past, and we thank 
you in advance for your assistance this time. 
A stamped, self-
addressed envelope is enclosed for your convenience. 
MGCjbh 
Encl 
Cooper 
Director 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Melvin G. Cooper 
Executive Director 
Alabama state Ethics 
Commission 
March 16, 1988 
817 South Court Street, Suite 28 Annex 
Montgomery, AL 
36104 
Re: 
88-105 
Dear Mr. Cooper: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on March 11, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact John McLean, an attorney in the 
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 
18329) .) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Diane M. 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • 
(916)322-5660
